explanations.3: The DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive flag tells the package installer not to ask interactive questions, so the build doesn't hang waiting for user input.
explanations.5: This removes files that indicate the system needs to reboot, which isn't applicable inside a Docker container.
explanations.6: This tells the system which display manager to use by default — lightdm is the one that handles the graphical login screen.
explanations.10: The xremote type tells lightdm to use X11 graphics over the network instead of locally, connecting to the host machine's display.
explanations.11: This points lightdm to where the X server (the graphics system) is running — on your actual computer, not inside the container.
explanations.18: This environment variable tells applications where to send their graphics output, pointing to the host machine's display at display number 0.
explanations.20: This starts two background services when the container runs: dbus handles system messaging and lightdm provides the graphical login interface.
